Gut secretome × human-receptor structural screen
A mobile microbial peptide that binds a human immune receptor
A structure-first screen of the gut secretome surfaces MIMIC — an orphan, expressed, mobile-element-borne peptide that docks the IL-7 receptor at its cytokine site.
Kenneth Loi · Zhaojun Wang · analysis built with Claude Science

Human and microbial cells are roughly even — but microbial genes outnumber ours ~100 to 1.

humanmicrobial cellsmicrobial genes

Sender, Fuchs & Milo 2016 (cell counts); microbiome gene catalogs (coding potential).

1 / 23

We already know microbes signal to us — through small molecules.

microbial metabolitehuman receptor

SCFAs→GPR43/109A; bile acids→FXR/TGR5; tryptophan metabolites→AhR.

2 / 23

Some microbial molecules even mimic our own ligands.

human ligandmicrobial mimic

Cohen et al. 2017, Nature — commensal-encoded GPCR agonists.

3 / 23

We have studied the metabolites. The secreted proteins are the gap.

Small molecules are promiscuous; proteins can be specific, high-affinity, evolvable.

small moleculesecreted proteinreceptor

4 / 23

Our hypothesis: some secreted miniproteins were selected to engage human receptors.

effector criteriacandidate signal

Hypothesis prioritization, not causation.

5 / 23

Commensals encode ~10⁹ miniproteins. Four filters cut them to a defensible set.

all smORFsgut/secreted/HQfamily repsfold set

GMSC → gut + secreted/TM + high-quality → 2,187 families → 451 with no bacterial-signaling system.

6 / 23

Each peptide is co-folded against 15 human receptors and 10 decoy receptors.

peptidereceptor of interestdecoy receptor

Decoys = unrelated groove-bearing receptors; they define the null a real hit must beat.

7 / 23

Every peptide is co-folded against the full panel with ESMFold2, K=5 per pair.

peptidereceptordecoy

451 × 25 = 11,275 pairs; 71% reused from a prior core sweep. Same engine and scale throughout.

8 / 23

Raw iPTM cannot tell receptors from decoys — so raw score is not the signal.

receptor pairsdecoy pairs

Receptor and decoy iPTM distributions overlap (means 0.21 vs 0.21). A high raw iPTM = a sticky peptide, not a specific binder. This is why we calibrate against decoys.

9 / 23

A hit must beat its own decoy baseline and fold a confident interface.

receptordecoyTier-A hit

Gate: iPTM ≥ 0.5 AND interface PAE ≤ 15 Å AND decoy-calibrated z ≥ 3. 11 peptide families clear all three thresholds; every decoy pair falls below.

10 / 23

From a beautiful hit to a hard question — we audited our own screen.

max iPTM ≥ 0.7below 0.7

The first screen ranked on precursor-sequence folds. A provenance audit found four methodological flaws before we trusted any nomination.

11 / 23

Fold the mature peptide, not the precursor — and most hits collapse.

precursormaturemature survives gate

Signal peptides are cleaved on secretion. Re-folding the mature form drops 4 of 5 Tier-A pairs below the gate. The old hero (TLR2) falls from 0.91 to 0.40 — it was a signal-peptide artifact.

12 / 23

The corrected lead: a mature, expressed, orphan peptide on IL7R.

mature iPTMinterface PAEprotein-level expression

078_038_456 × IL7R passes every corrected axis: folded as the mature form (iPTM 0.91), detected at the protein level (metaP 0.64), no bacterial cognate, and a binding site that is accessible on the free receptor.

13 / 23

A gut peptide lands on the IL-7 binding site

Click through: IL7R → SCID residues → native IL-7 → MIMIC, same surface. Drag to rotate.

Click to build the interface
IL7R — the receptor
SCID residues — where immune-deficiency mutations cluster
native IL-7 — the natural cytokine, in its groove
MIMIC — the gut peptide, on the same surface
Co-location with the functional interface, not enrichment.
⬌ drag to rotate · scroll to zoom
gut peptide 078native IL-7IL7RSCID variant residues
14 / 23

It picks IL-7Rα out of its own receptor family

A generic sticker would hit the paralogs too. It doesn't — two independent engines agree.

What the bars show
IL-7Rα — the real target: a strong, confident interface
the 5 closest paralogs (its own γc family) — no real interface
The decoys tested "generic groove-binder." This tests the hardest case — the five most similar receptors — and MIMIC clears them all. AlphaFold3 agrees independently.
IL-7Rα (target)γc paralogs
15 / 23

Two independent specificity filters — and the lead passes both.

Broad hit counts favor other peptides — but ortholog quality (length match + operon synteny) singles out the hero.

Accessibility: the peptide footprint is compared to the surface buried by each receptor's native partner in an experimental complex. Cognate: each dual-context peptide is folded against its own bacterial sensor kinase. Human-receptor iPTM 0.79 vs bacterial-cognate 0.21.

hero (sweet spot)widespreadnear-singleton

MMseqs iterative search vs UniProtKB. Next: test whether MEP-operon synteny AND the TLR2 interface are conserved across genera — the signature of selection.

16 / 23

Orphan candidates sit in conserved operon slots across distant genera.

secreted peptide (281_962_460)ispFispDother (unlabeled)

8 representatives shown of 19 Oscillospiraceae genera (26–44% peptide identity). The MEP-isoprenoid operon (ispD–ispF–peptide) is syntenically conserved — a selected genomic module, not a near-neighbor artifact. Loci recovered via UniProt→EMBL, rendered with LoVis4u.

17 / 23

The peptide rides a mobile element into a pathogen

Same Tcp/ArdA conjugative element, same peptide, across 239 gut species — including 15 Clostridioides difficile strains.

p = 0.003
MGE enrichment
(#2 of 592 families)
239 species
Bacillota + Actinomycetota
= horizontal transfer
C. difficile
colitis pathogen
15 strains carry it
peptideconjugationintegrase/ISArdA
18 / 23

A shield you can trade between bugs

MIMIC — Mobile, ImmunoModulatory, ICE-borne, Commensal. Leading hypothesis (of two): it mutes IL-7 to keep its microbe immune-invisible — a trait the mobile element can trade.

Test it in a week
add MIMIC + IL-7 on T cells → read pSTAT5 · suppressed = antagonist/shield · drives it alone = agonist
commensalpathogen (C. difficile)
19 / 23

From one skeptical question to a re-run screen — how this was built.

The insight was human. The execution — audit, refold, rerank, specificity filters — was agent-run, end to end, in one session.

20 / 23

The assembled hypothesis: an HGT-borne peptide that may quiet IL-7 signaling.

Five independent lines of evidence converge on one candidate — 078_038_456 × IL7R.

Structurally supported
mature iPTM 0.91 · beats decoy null (z=6)
IL-7-competitive
docks the cytokine site · 65% overlap · likely antagonist
Disease-anchored
SCID variant cluster at the epitope
Mobile / HGT
ICE cargo in 4/5 genomes · p=0.003
Expressed
protein-level metaproteomic detection
A gut commensal carries, on a conjugative element traded between species, a peptide predicted to occupy the IL-7 binding site of IL7R — potentially blunting a lymphocyte-homeostasis signal at the surface where human SCID mutations cluster. A candidate microbial immune-modulator.
What this is not (yet): co-folding gives interface geometry, not agonist-vs-antagonist direction; "immune evasion" is a hypothesis, not a measured function. Next: a binding/reporter assay and a complex co-fold.
structuremechanismdiseasemobility
21 / 23

This is a prioritized, structurally-supported hypothesis — not proof of binding.

disease contextgut signalingtherapeutic opening

Ceiling: sequence-only folding misses lipopeptide/glycan chemistry; interface prediction ≠ affinity. Confirmation = complex co-fold + shuffled-decoy FDR + wet-lab binding.

22 / 23
The gut may encode a whole library of human-receptor ligands — waiting in microbial genomes to be read.
Open-source · reproducible · Kenneth Loi, Zhaojun Wang · Built with Claude
